summary: A Ukrainian drone strike hit the middle superstructure of the Russian frigate "Admiral Essen" in Novorossiysk Bay, detonating PK-10 passive barrier grenade launchers and damaging the TK-25 electronic warfare complex. Ukrainian operators conducted FPV strikes 50+ km into occupied Donetsk, targeting military and logistics vehicles. The Rubicon Center intercepted over 1,500 Ukrainian fixed-wing UAVs, with 75% belonging to 11 models including Leleka-100 and Fury. Russian forces destroyed 107 Ukrainian targets on March 6, 2026, across multiple directions. Ukrainian firm Vyriy developed new "Lightning" UAV variants with retargeting capabilities. Ukrainian forces are now deploying fixed-wing UAVs carrying two FPV drones each, prompting calls for enhanced anti-drone defenses. Iranian Navy's Shahid Bagheri drone carrier was likely destroyed in a US-Israeli strike. Russian forces used FAB-3000 bombs with UMPK modules, guided by fiber-optic FPV drones, to strike Ukrainian positions in Krasny Liman. ZALA Lancet operators successfully hit group targets despite electronic warfare interference.
